snippets.title: Batch GD by hand on a quadratic loss
snippets.explanation: The core update: subtract η times the gradient. On this convex loss, w converges toward the minimum at 5.

snippets.title: Full-batch GD loop in PyTorch
snippets.code:
import torch
import torch.nn as nn

# Toy: all data in one batch (full-batch GD)
X = torch.tensor([[1.0], [2.0], [3.0]])
y = torch.tensor([[2.0], [4.0], [6.0]])

model = nn.Linear(1, 1)
optimizer = torch.optim.SGD(model.parameters(), lr=0.1)
criterion = nn.MSELoss()

for epoch in range(50):
    optimizer.zero_grad()
    pred = model(X)                    # forward on full dataset
    loss = criterion(pred, y)
    loss.backward()                    # grad L over all samples
    optimizer.step()                   # theta <- theta - eta * grad L

print("w, b:", model.weight.item(), model.bias.item())
snippets.explanation: Passing the entire dataset in one forward pass makes each `step()` a batch GD update. `zero_grad()` clears stale gradients before each backward.

snippets.title: Average per-example gradients equals full-batch gradient
snippets.code:
import torch

w = torch.tensor(2.0, requires_grad=True)

# Per-example squared errors: L_i = (w - y_i)^2
y = torch.tensor([1.0, 3.0, 5.0])
per_example = [(w - yi) ** 2 for yi in y]

# Full-batch loss: mean of per-example losses
L_batch = torch.stack(per_example).mean()
L_batch.backward()
grad_batch = w.grad.clone()

w.grad = None
# Manual average of per-example gradients
grads = []
for Li in per_example:
    Li.backward(retain_graph=True)
    grads.append(w.grad.clone())
    w.grad = None
grad_mean = torch.stack(grads).mean()

print("grad from mean loss:", grad_batch.item())
print("mean of per-example grads:", grad_mean.item())
snippets.explanation: For average loss, ∇L equals the average of ∇Lᵢ—the mathematical basis for accumulating gradients over a full batch.
